sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 15.62 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.15)

statement.latex: Given an ordinary irreducible character$$of a finite group$G$write$p^{e_p( )}$to denote the$p$-part of$(1)$and put$e_p(G)= \{e_p( ) (G)\}$. Suppose that$P$is a Sylow$p$-sub\-group of a group$G$. Is it true that$e_p(P)$is bounded above by a function of$e_p(G)$? Comment of 2005: An affirmative answer in the case of solvable groups has been given in (A.\,Moret\'o, T.\,R.\,Wolf, Adv. Math., 184 (2004)...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
